methods.description: Process for growing diatom cultures under controlled light conditions to prepare samples for further analysis.

steps.description: Three 100-150 μmol photons m −2 s −1 (Panasonic FL40ss Enw/37, Japan) with a light/dark cycle of 16 h/8 h without aeration.
steps.searchable_summary: grow diatom cultures under specified light conditions

steps.description: The growth medium was L1 medium with a salinity of 30 prepared with autoclaved seawater.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare L1 growth medium with specified salinity

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing the morphology of diatom frustules using Transmission Electron Microscopy (TEM).

steps.description: Near the end of the exponential growth stage (around 10 days), a subsample of each of the three diatom species were acid cleaned to remove organic material.
steps.searchable_summary: clean diatom samples with acid to remove organic material

steps.description: The cleaned frustule material was drop-casted onto copper grids, and examined using a JEM-1010 (Jeol, Japan) Transmission Electron Microscope (TEM).
Frustule diameter, foramen number in 10 µm (foramen density) and the diameter of the foramen were measured on 20-26 frustules from each species and average values were calculated.
steps.searchable_summary: measure and calculate average frustule dimensions

methods.method_name: UV Light Transparency and Reflectance Measurement
methods.description: Method for measuring UV light transparency, reflectance, and absorbance of diatom frustules using a spectrophotometer.

steps.description: Drops (around 10 µl) of concentrated solutions of rinsed frustules were dripped onto the measuring-point of a light-transparent quartz silica microscope glass plate and dried at room temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare frustule samples on quartz silica plates

steps.description: Microscope pictures were taken using an Olympus GX41 light microscope to calculate the layer coverage of the measuring-point.
steps.searchable_summary: capture microscope images for layer coverage calculation

steps.description: Transmittance (T) and Reflectance (R) spectra were measured using a Shimadzu UV-2600 spectrometer.
Absorbance (A) was calculated based on the equation: A = 100 -(T + R).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate absorbance from transmittance and reflectance

methods.description: Technique for capturing 3D morphology and depth information of diatom frustule layers using an Alicona Infinite Focus Microscope.

steps.description: The surface topography of the layer was measured using an Alicona Infinite Focus Microscope.
The IFM measurement was analyzed with a software processor package to characterize the thickness of the coated film.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze IFM data to determine film thickness

methods.method_name: Wide Angle X-ray Scattering Setup and Modeling
methods.description: Procedure for measuring and modeling small-and wide-angle X-ray scattering profiles of diatom samples.

steps.description: Small-and Wide-angle X-ray scattering profiles were measured using a GANESHA instrument.
steps.searchable_summary: measure SAXS/WAXS profiles with GANESHA instrument

steps.description: The two-dimensional scattering data were azimuthally averaged and corrected for detector inhomogeneities using standard reduction software.
steps.searchable_summary: process scattering data with azimuthal averaging

steps.description: The SAXS data are analyzed using the unified Beaucage model.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Beaucage model to SAXS data

steps.description: Data and model agreement was optimized using standard least-squares fitting done in Matlab.
steps.searchable_summary: optimize model fit with least-squares in Matlab
